highlights.text: Users are more tolerant of minor usability issues when they find an interface visually appealing. This aesthetic-usability effect can mask UI problems during usability testing. Identify instances of the aesthetic-usability effect in your user research by watching what your users do, as well as listening to what they say.

A user starts a session on any webpage → gets a shareable session link
Other users join via that link → everyone's text highlights and cursor positions sync in real time over WebSocket
A shared sidebar shows a conversation thread with an AI Socratic partner (Claude)
The AI asks questions — it never summarizes or completes work for you
